prompt: Read the sandbox hello file and tell me what it says.
Read the sandbox hello file and tell me what it says.
List the current workspace root and summarize the important directories and files for a coding agent.

context.task: List the current workspace root and summarize the important directories and files for a coding agent.
context.workspace_rule: Use only relative paths in the MCP workspace

raw:
*Thinking:*  I'm noticing the input format has shifted back to the planner style with just the task, available tools, and workspace rules. Since I'm operating as the planner here, I need to generate the planning JSON response, though I've already done substantial exploration on this workspace summary task.

{"status":"done","goal":"Enumerate the workspace root and produce a coding-agent-oriented summary that buckets every top-level entry by role (entry points, config, source modules, instrumentation, tests, scripts, docs, runtime artifacts, ignore) with concrete evidence from file contents or directory listings.","exploration_checks":["Call list_directory('.') to capture every top-level entry with [DIR]/[FILE] markers and count","Read project metadata files to confirm stack: read_file on README.md, requirements.txt, Dockerfile, VERSION","Read entry-point candidates (head only, ~60 lines each): start.py, mcp_server.py, login.py, start.sh — extract shebang, docstring, and first imports","List each non-hidden source subdirectory once: list_directory on app/, unified/, worker/, chatbai/, skillboss/, therouter/, wavespeed/, backup_gumloop_cli/, docs/","Resolve extensionless root files via file_info on 'ns' and 'unifiedme'","Treat .git/, .venv/, .sisyphus/, .mcp_server.log as tooling/ignore — do NOT descend into them"],"execution_steps":["Run list_directory('.') and record all entries","For README.md, requirements.txt, Dockerfile, VERSION: read_file and extract project name, Python version, key dependencies (FastAPI? Flask? CLI?), declared purpose","For start.py, mcp_server.py, login.py, start.sh: read_file with limit~60 to capture docstring/shebang/first imports","For each top-level source directory in turn (app, unified, worker, chatbai, skillboss, therouter, wavespeed, backup_gumloop_cli, docs): list_directory and capture top entries to infer role","Call file_info on 'ns' and on 'unifiedme' to determine size and whether they are scripts, binaries, or stray text files","Bucket every one of the 66 root entries into exactly one category: entry-points, config/deploy, source packages, instrumentation/interception, tests, utility scripts, documentation, runtime artifacts, environment/tooling (ignore)","Compose the final summary: project name & stack (from README + requirements), main entry points (with paths), source-directory map (with observed contents), instrumentation cluster, docs cluster, tests cluster, ignore list — explicitly flag any item still not directly inspected"],"validation_checks":["Every one of the 66 top-level entries appears in exactly one bucket in the final summary","Each bucket assignment is backed by file contents read or directory listing observed — items assigned from filename alone are explicitly tagged 'inferred'","Project stack (language, framework, deploy target) is stated based on README.md + requirements.txt contents, not just filenames","'ns' and 'unifiedme' are resolved via file_info (size + type) or flagged as still unknown","Any subdirectory that was not list_directory'd is named explicitly as un-inspected — no fabricated role descriptions","Summary clearly distinguishes confirmed facts (read/listed) from inferred facts (named-only)"]}

plan.goal: Enumerate the workspace root and produce a coding-agent-oriented summary that buckets every top-level entry by role (entry points, config, source modules, instrumentation, tests, scripts, docs, runtime artifacts, ignore) with concrete evidence from file contents or directory listings.
exploration_checks: Call list_directory('.') to capture every top-level entry with [DIR]/[FILE] markers and count
Read project metadata files to confirm stack: read_file on README.md, requirements.txt, Dockerfile, VERSION
Read entry-point candidates (head only, ~60 lines each): start.py, mcp_server.py, login.py, start.sh — extract shebang, docstring, and first imports
List each non-hidden source subdirectory once: list_directory on app/, unified/, worker/, chatbai/, skillboss/, therouter/, wavespeed/, backup_gumloop_cli/, docs/
Resolve extensionless root files via file_info on 'ns' and 'unifiedme'
Treat .git/, .venv/, .sisyphus/, .mcp_server.log as tooling/ignore — do NOT descend into them
execution_steps: Run list_directory('.') and record all entries
For README.md, requirements.txt, Dockerfile, VERSION: read_file and extract project name, Python version, key dependencies (FastAPI? Flask? CLI?), declared purpose
For start.py, mcp_server.py, login.py, start.sh: read_file with limit~60 to capture docstring/shebang/first imports
For each top-level source directory in turn (app, unified, worker, chatbai, skillboss, therouter, wavespeed, backup_gumloop_cli, docs): list_directory and capture top entries to infer role
Call file_info on 'ns' and on 'unifiedme' to determine size and whether they are scripts, binaries, or stray text files

Bucket every one of the 66 root entries into exactly one category: entry-points, config/deploy, source packages, instrumentation/interception, tests, utility scripts, documentation, runtime artifacts, environment/tooling (ignore)

Compose the final summary: project name & stack (from README + requirements), main entry points (with paths), source-directory map (with observed contents), instrumentation cluster, docs cluster, tests cluster, ignore list — explicitly flag any item still not directly inspected
validation_checks: Every one of the 66 top-level entries appears in exactly one bucket in the final summary
Each bucket assignment is backed by file contents read or directory listing observed — items assigned from filename alone are explicitly tagged 'inferred'
Project stack (language, framework, deploy target) is stated based on README.md + requirements.txt contents, not just filenames
'ns' and 'unifiedme' are resolved via file_info (size + type) or flagged as still unknown
Any subdirectory that was not list_directory'd is named explicitly as un-inspected — no fabricated role descriptions
Summary clearly distinguishes confirmed facts (read/listed) from inferred facts (named-only)

raw: {"status":"needs_tool","tool":"list_directory","arguments":{"path":"."},"reason":"Begin by enumerating every top-level entry in the workspace root so each can be bucketed against the plan."}

action.reason: Begin by enumerating every top-level entry in the workspace root so each can be bucketed against the plan.

raw: {"status":"needs_tool","tool":"read_file","arguments":{"path":"README.md","limit":80},"reason":"Root enumeration done (66 entries). Next: confirm project identity, stack, and purpose by reading the top of README.md before bucketing entries."}

action.reason: Root enumeration done (66 entries). Next: confirm project identity, stack, and purpose by reading the top of README.md before bucketing entries.

result.text:
1: # Unified AI Proxy
2: 
3: Single OpenAI-compatible endpoint that routes to multiple AI providers with account rotation, credit tracking, and centralized license management.
4: 
5: ## Providers
6: 
7: | Tier | Provider | Models |
8: |------|----------|--------|
9: | Standard | Kiro (AWS Q) | `claude-sonnet-4`, `deepseek-3.2`, `qwen3-coder-next` |
10: | Max | CodeBuddy | `claude-opus-4.6`, `gpt-5.4`, `gemini-2.5-pro` |
11: | WaveSpeed | WaveSpeed LLM | `new-claude-opus-4.7`, `new-gpt-5.2` |
12: | Max GL | Gumloop | `gl-claude-opus-4-7`, `gl-gpt-5.4` |
13: 
14: ## Quick Install
15: 
16: ```bash
17: curl -sSL https://unified-api.roubot71.workers.dev/install | bash
18: ```
19: 
20: Requirements: Python 3.10+, Git, curl.
21: 
22: ## Commands
23: 
24: ```bash
25: unifiedme run          # Start proxy (foreground, interactive)
26: unifiedme start        # Start proxy (background daemon)
27: unifiedme stop         # Stop background proxy
28: unifiedme status       # Show status, version, uptime
29: unifiedme update       # Pull latest code + reinstall deps
30: unifiedme kill-port    # Kill zombie process on port 1430
31: unifiedme logout       # Clear license key (switch license)
32: unifiedme help         # Show help
33: ```
34: 
35: ## First Time Setup
36: 
37: ```
38: 1. unifiedme run
39: 2. Enter your license key (UNIF-XXXXX-XXXXX-XXXXX-XXXXX-XXXXX)
40: 3. Open http://localhost:1430/dashboard
41: 4. Set your admin password
42: 5. Copy your API key (sk-xxx)
43: 6. Use in your IDE/CLI:
44:    Authorization: Bearer sk-xxx
45: ```
46: 
47: ## API Endpoints
48: 
49: ```
50: POST /v1/chat/completions    OpenAI-compatible chat (routes by model)
51: POST /v1/messages            Anthropic-compatible messages
52: GET  /v1/models              List available models
53: GET  /dashboard              Admin dashboard
54: ```
55: 
56: ## Architecture
57: 
58: ```
59: Client (IDE/CLI)
60:   |
61:   POST /v1/chat/completions
62:   Authorization: Bearer sk-xxx
63:   |
64: Unified Proxy (:1430)
65:   |-- Model Router (by model name -> tier)
66:   |-- Account Manager (sticky rotation, auto-ban)
67:   |-- Message Filter (find/replace before upstream)
68:   |-- Watchword Scanner (alert on keywords)
69:   |
70:   |-- Kiro Proxy     -> AWS Q API (direct Python)
71:   |-- CodeBuddy Proxy -> codebuddy.ai
72:   |-- WaveSpeed Proxy -> wavespeed.ai
73:   |-- Gumloop Proxy   -> WebSocket + Firebase auth
74:   |
75:   |-- License Client  -> Central D1 Database
76:       (sync accounts, usage logs, alerts every 5 min)
77: ```
78: 
79: ## License System
80:
